label: Wide 2BR / 1 bath — standard bedroom front-left (narrower), common T&B as horizontal mid-band slab, master bedroom rear-left (wider + deeper); wide great room front-right. Master is larger than standard per PH convention.

adjacencies.note: standard's east wall = great's west wall — vertical door (vr: standard.x1 == great.x0). Vertical overlap == standard.depth >= 1.2 m. Standard is front-left; the shared vertical wall at x=standard.x1 is the column divider for all three left-column rooms.
standard's south wall = common's north wall — horizontal shared wall, no door (hr: standard.y1 == common.y0). Acoustic buffer; single_bath access is standard -> great -> common.

common's east wall = great's west wall — bath door (vr: common.x1 == great.x0 == standard.x1). Both standard and common share the same vertical wall at x=S with the great room; common's portion hosts the T&B door.
common's south wall = master's north wall — horizontal shared wall, no door (hr: common.y1 == master.y0). Acoustic buffer; single_bath access is master -> great -> common.

master's north wall overlaps great's south wall — horizontal door (hf: master.y0 == great.y1 == y2). Overlap == master.x1 - great.x0 == master.width - standard.width >= 0.8 m. Master is wider than standard, so its east portion extends into the great room's x-range.
open LDK — kitchen's north wall = great's south wall (hf: kitchen.y0 == great.y1 == y2). Kitchen and master share the rear zone at y2..ey1.

soft_proximities.note: Wet-stack proximity: pulls common (mid-left) and kitchen (rear-right) close to reduce plumbing run length.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_2br_kitchen_shape
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Floor kitchen width at 2.0 m on compact envelopes.

notes: Wide-shell catalog topology — 2BR / 1 bath with common T&B as a HORIZONTAL MID-BAND
slab; standard at FRONT-LEFT, master at REAR-LEFT (wider + deeper).
WHY FLIPPED: In the wide-shell column layout standard must be WIDER than master
(so standard.x1 overhangs master.x1 to give standard a door into the great room).
But the solver enforces area[master] >= area[standard] + 1 sqm globally. If standard
is wider, master can only win on DEPTH. The mid-band common bath (not rear-anchored)
redistributes depth: standard is shallow (front, ~2.1-2.5 m), common is ~0.9-1.2 m,
master is deep (rear, ~3-3.5 m) AND wider. Master easily dominates on both axes.
The shared vertical wall at x=standard.x1=common.x1=great.x0 serves three functions:
standard's east door to great (bedroom_to_public, vertical overlap = std.depth)
common's east bath door to great (bath_door, vertical overlap = common.depth)
master's horizontal door to great at y=y2 (hf: master.y0 = great.y1, overlap = master.width - standard.width)
Single_bath access rule maintained: BOTH bedrooms reach common via the great room.
standard -> great -> common
master   -> great -> common
No bedroom accesses the bath directly (no bedroom-to-bath door).
Layout intent (NCP, no carport):
Front (y=0..y1):    [standard (left, narrow) | great (right, wide)           ]
Mid   (y=y1..y2):   [common   (left, slab)   | great (right, continues)      ]
Rear  (y=y2..ey1):  [master   (left, wide)   | kitchen (right)               ]
Feasibility window on 6 m buildable depth: standard.depth (y1) >= ~2.1 m keeps the
aspect-ratio and zone-ratio constraints simultaneously satisfiable. Confirmed feasible
on 13x10 (9x6 m, aspect 1.5) and 14x10 (10x6 m, aspect 1.67) NCP briefs.
